name: rules
context: Enable, disable, and configure ESLint rules — severities, options, per-file overrides, and inline directives
info: This skill covers the full rule management lifecycle in ESLint: setting rule severities (off/warn/error), configuring rule options, applying per-file overrides with files/ignores patterns, using inline directives (eslint-disable comments), and working with recommended rule sets. Agents use this skill when fixing lint violations, enabling stricter rules, suppressing rules for specific files or lines, and understanding what each rule enforces. See [[eslint/config]] for overall configuration setup and [[eslint/plugins]] for plugin-specific rules like @typescript-eslint/* and react/*.
triggers: turn off eslint rule for a file
what does this eslint rule do

sections.title: Rule severities
sections.body: Three levels: `"off"` (0) disables the rule, `"warn"` (1) reports without affecting exit code, `"error"` (2) reports and causes exit code 1. Use `"error"` for CI enforcement, `"warn"` for soft guidance. Array form `["error", options]` passes additional configuration to the rule.

sections.title: Inline disable directives
sections.body: Use `// eslint-disable-next-line <rule>` to suppress a rule on the next line. Use `/* eslint-disable <rule> */` and `/* eslint-enable <rule> */` for blocks. Use `/* eslint-disable <rule> */` at the top of a file to disable for the entire file. Always specify the rule name — avoid blanket `eslint-disable` without a rule. Add explanations with `--` separator.

sections.title: Per-file overrides
sections.body: Each config object in the flat config array can specify `files` globs to target specific files. Later config objects override earlier ones for matching files. Common pattern: strict rules globally, relaxed rules for tests and scripts. The `files` property accepts arrays of glob patterns.

sections.title: Common rules reference
sections.body: Key rules: `no-unused-vars` (flag unused variables, configure with `argsIgnorePattern: "^_"`), `eqeqeq` (require strict equality), `prefer-const` (use const when not reassigned), `no-console` (disallow console), `semi` (enforce semicolons), `quotes` (enforce quote style). Check fixability: rules that are fixable respond to `--fix`. See [[eslint/fix]] for auto-fixing.
